title: [makefile] - /bin/sh: c: command not found
body:
```
$ make help
/bin/sh: c: command not found
make: [Makefile:140: help] Error 127 (ignored)
```

comments.body:
Works for me on Fedora 33
```
make help
Usage: make <target>
 * 'binaries' - Build podman
 * 'changelog' - Generate changelog
 * 'clean' - Clean artifacts
 * 'docs' - Generate documentation
 * 'gofmt' - Verify the source code gofmt
 * 'install' - Install binaries to system locations
 * 'integration' - Execute integration tests
 * 'libpodimage' - Build the libpod image
 * 'local-cross' - Cross local compilation
 * 'package' - Build rpm packages
 * 'package-install' - Install rpm packages
 * 'shell' - Run the built image and attach a shell
 * 'test' - Run tests on built image
 * 'testunit' - Run unittest on the built image
 * 'varlink_generate' - Generate varlink
```

```
[root@Centos8 ~]# cd podman
[root@Centos8 ~/podman]# make help
/bin/sh: c: command not found
make: [Makefile:140: help] Error 127 (ignored)
[root@Centos8 ~/podman]# make --version
GNU Make 4.2.1
Built for x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu
Copyright (C) 1988-2016 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
License GPLv3+: GNU GPL version 3 or later <http://gnu.org/licenses/gpl.html>
This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it.
There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law.
[root@Centos8 ~/podman]# cat /etc/redhat-release 
CentOS Linux release 8.2.2004 (Core)
```

Most likely you don't have python installed ?

```Makefile
PYTHON ?= $(shell command -v python3 python|head -n1)
help:
        @$(PYTHON) -c "$$PRINT_HELP_PYSCRIPT" < $(MAKEFILE_LIST)
```

Reproducer:
```
 make help PYTHON=""
/bin/sh: 1: c: not found
make: [Makefile:140: help] Error 127 (ignored)

```

> Most likely you don't have python installed ?
> 
> ```makefile
> PYTHON ?= $(shell command -v python3 python|head -n1)
> help:
>         @$(PYTHON) -c "$$PRINT_HELP_PYSCRIPT" < $(MAKEFILE_LIST)
> ```
> 
> Reproducer:
> 
> ```
>  make help PYTHON=""
> /bin/sh: 1: c: not found
> make: [Makefile:140: help] Error 127 (ignored)
> ```

yes, maybe need a nice remind in the Makefile
Please open a PR to fix this.
My knowledge of makefile is very weak

@edsantiago @nalind Do you have any ideas.  I tried some stuff out but hit a roadblock.

I was thinking something like:

```
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 253207f49..a25c900e8 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -135,8 +135,13 @@ export PRINT_HELP_PYSCRIPT
 err_if_empty = $(if $(strip $($(1))),$(strip $($(1))),$(error Required variable $(1) value is undefined, whitespace, or empty))
 
 .PHONY: help
+ifneq ("", ${PYTHON})
 help:
        @$(PYTHON) -c "$$PRINT_HELP_PYSCRIPT" < $(MAKEFILE_LIST)
+else
+help:
+       @echo python executable not found, required for "make help"
+endif
 
 .gopathok:
 ifeq ("$(wildcard $(GOPKGDIR))","")
```
But I can not get it to work properly.

Or just set `PYTHON=python`, if python3 is not found ?

But then it won't have the friendly message, just CNF
